📱 Coming from another device?

Your account already exists on your other device · paste your transfer code here to link this device to it. Generate the code on your existing device under Settings → Devices → "Sign in on another device".

How cross-device works in this build:
Static HTML can't authenticate across devices without a backend. Use the transfer code above to link a new device — one-time per device, then sign-in works normally.

For production cross-device, wire a backend at window.WIREIP_AUTH_BACKEND_URL · see WIREIP-AUTH-BACKEND.md.

iAbout / Specs
⌬ WireIP Network™ · Settlement Rail v4.1

The Plumbing of Intellectual Property Value

WireIP is the foundational protocol for intellectual property ownership, verification, and settlement · built on an asynchronous BFT DAG, anchored by Proof of Agency™, and backed by $WIP and the OTW Treasury's hard-asset reserves. Creators tokenize their IP as CBIA™ Digital Deeds, establish immutable on-chain provenance, and access stablecoin liquidity against verified real-world assets.

Royalty splits, licensing, cross-border IP commerce, and institutional lending all settle through the same sovereign infrastructure · with the throughput of a card network and the custody standards of a digital commodity. In a stablecoin failure, $WIP deploys as the last-resort backstop · par redemption for every tokenized creator.

SETTLEMENT RAILS USDC USDT USDP $WIP · BACKSTOP MULTI-STABLECOIN COMPLIANCE FIPS 140-2 L3 NIST SP 800-53 SOC 2 TYPE II
1/1 MASTER
ABSOLUTE MASTER TOKEN · ORIGINAL OF ONE ⚠ NOT AN NFT
Every mint issues a single, sovereign 1/1 Master Token · the original, irreproducible Digital Deed of your intellectual property. Fractional royalty tokens (when enabled below) are derivatives · they reference the Master, they do not replace it. These are Tokenized IP Assets with Real-World Value, court-recognizable, leverage-eligible, and backed by hard treasury reserves · not collectibles, not JPEGs, not speculation.

Issue a CBIA™ Digital Deed

Issue and settle a CBIA™ Digital Deed for your IP on the WireIP DAG. The file is hashed locally, encrypted (AES-256), anchored to your SecureNode™ SSD vault, and routed through stablecoin settlement rails · clearing in ~4–8 seconds (soft final) and finalizing in ~12–20 seconds (hard final). Per protocol, all issuance requires Proof of Agency™ via NFC tap. After mint, deploy to vault only or have AI Sentinel agents settle the deed with selected national IP offices.

Upload and Connect

Bring an existing tokenized asset into your vault from a connected wallet, or upload a fresh file to begin a new CBIA™ Digital Deed. Either path hashes locally, encrypts to your SecureNode™ SSD vault, and prepares the asset for mint.
1

Upload Asset File

No file
⬆
Drop file here or click to browse
Image · Video · Audio · PDF · Document · Code · Any format · < 2 GB
File is hashed in your browser (SHA-256 → BLAKE3-style CBIA payload). Original bytes are sent encrypted to SecureNode™ · they never leave your device unencrypted.
⌬

Originality & AI Provenance Check

Standby
Sentinel agents screen the asset against the WireIP global infringement index, run AI-generation detection on the bytes, validate EXIF / file metadata for AI-tool fingerprints (DALL·E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, GPT, Sora, etc.), and compare perceptual hash against your existing vault.

Asset ID Visualizer

Asset name + hex color + creator → unique visual ID
Every asset gets a unique visual ID built from three identifiers you control: the asset name shown on the canvas, a color hex code (drag the waveform or paste a hex), and a procedural pattern seed (16M+ unique patterns). Combined with your creator name, this generates a deterministic Asset ID for the deed certificate, vault cards, search results, and DAG vertex.

⚜

IP Ownership Proof · qualifies asset for leverage

Unqualified for leverage
Upload an authentic registration document (US Copyright Office certificate, USPTO Patent Grant, USPTO Trademark Registration, EUIPO / WIPO / UKIPO certificate, assignment, or chain-of-title document) to prove you legally own this IP. AGI Sentinel cross-checks the registration number, owner name, and validity dates. Verified ownership unlocks loan-against-asset (leverage) eligibility.
⚜
Drop registration certificate or grant document
Copyright Office cert · USPTO grant · TM registration · WIPO · EUIPO · assignments · PDF / JPG / PNG
○ No ownership proof attached. Asset can still be minted but will not be leverage-qualified.

Tokenization Parameters

Proof-of-Value · Real-World Valuation $WIP  $0.85  
Initial Stake reflects the asset's verifiable real-world value. Add proof items below · revenue statements, licenses, court judgments, streaming counts, royalty reports · and run an AI Authenticity Scan on each. The AI reads the document text, looks for digital + physical manipulation tells (compression artifacts, copy-paste edges, EXIF tampering, OCR mismatch), and assigns a confidence score. Only verified value counts toward the stake.
No proof yet · add your first Proof-of-Value item below.

Stake Ratio 100%
100% = stake matches the asset's USD value 1:1 at the live $WIP price (default). Slide down for a partial-collateral margin model.

→ Stake in $WIP 0 $WIP

⚔
AI Sentinel Soldier™ · Auto-File to IP Office Scan · analyze · submit · return proof of entry
Enable an AI Sentinel Soldier (AI Agent) to automatically scan and analyze this asset, then submit it to your local jurisdictional IP office on your behalf · U.S. Copyright Office, USPTO Patent / Trademark, WIPO, EPO, UKIPO, CIPO, JPO, KIPO, or CNIPA. The Sentinel handles the filing, pays the fee from your wallet, tracks the case, and returns a signed proof-of-entry receipt with the official case number anchored to your CBIA™ Deed.
Your Local Jurisdiction
Target Office (Auto-Selected by Type)
✓
Scan asset bytes & metadata (EXIF, AI-fingerprints, PHash)
✓
Auto-fill registration application from your asset details
✓
Submit to office & pay filing fee (stablecoin from wallet)
✓
Track case · respond to examiner · confirm registration
✓
Return signed Proof-of-Entry™ receipt to your vault
✓
Anchor official case # to your CBIA™ Digital Deed on-chain
Off: The deed mints to your sovereign WireIP vault only. You can still dispatch a Sentinel later from Deploy & Register. On: Auto-dispatch begins immediately after mint · first proof-of-entry typically returns in 24–72 hours.
6

Proof of Agency™ & Mint

PoA pending
Per protocol, all minting requires NFC tap on your SecureCard™. If SecureNode is connected, this issues a real challenge. Otherwise, simulated PoA is recorded.
⚖ Fees to Mint & Register $0

Office filing fees are paid in stablecoin from your linked wallet at the time of submission. Network fees ($WIP gas + DAG anchor) settle in $WIP. Estimates only · official fees may change.

Deploy & Register

Deed minted · ready to deploy
Your CBIA™ Deed is minted on the WireIP DAG. Choose how to deploy it:

Option A · Vault Only

Keep the deed sovereign on your SecureNode™ SSD vault. No external registry. Maximum privacy. Always reversible.

Option B · AI Sentinel Submission

Sentinel Soldiers™ submit your deed automatically to selected national IP offices. They handle filings, fees, and tracking on your behalf.

Select IP Offices

AI Sentinel agents are dispatched in parallel · one per office. Choose any combination:

Treasury & Real-World Value

Aggregate real-world valuation of every CBIA™ deed in your vault. Valuations are sourced from court-verified judgements, streaming royalties (auto-pulled by AGI Sentinel from connected platforms), licensing deals, professional appraisals, and on-chain comparables. LTV (Loan-to-Value) is shown live for any leveraged asset.

$WIP Tokenomics & Treasury

Real-time view of $WIP · the WireIP Network's underlying coin · and the Treasury's diversified hard-asset backing. $WIP has a fixed maximum supply of 100 million units. The Treasury holds gold, T-Bills, real estate, energy, cryptocurrencies, sovereign debt, and physical infrastructure to back tokenized IP value and stand by as a backstop in the event of stablecoin failure.

Analytics

Private Networks & Collectives

Create your own private network · a collective of creators, labels, studios, art centers and validators that operate as a sub-DAG within WireIP. Record companies join as nodes; studios and art centers act as validators. Royalties, governance, and licensing flow inside the network's perimeter.

Collaborators

Add creators, co-authors, producers, agents, lawyers, and partners to your collaboration roster. Collaborators can be tagged on minted CBIA™ deeds and added as members of your private networks. They appear in the left sidebar for quick access.

Messages

Direct chat between WireIP users · same-device tabs work out of the box. To talk to other people around the world, set a WebSocket relay URL in Settings → Cross-Device Sync. Everyone using the same relay URL can find and message each other.
Select a conversation to start

⌬ Admin · Network Observer

Master-account view of all traffic broadcast on this relay. This surface shows only metadata that users explicitly publish — presence pings, posted events, deployed assets, marketplace activity, message envelopes (sender/recipient, NOT body). Asset payloads, encrypted vault contents, and private profile fields are encrypted to each user's SecureNode and are inaccessible by design.
Observed users
0
Events observed
0
Assets deployed
0
Messages relayed
0

Live event stream · read-only

Architectural note. WireIP is a per-user-encrypted system: every user's IP assets, vault contents, and private profile fields live on THEIR device, encrypted with keys that only their SecureCard can unlock. The relay is a pub/sub channel for public broadcasts — not a data store. There is no technical path by which an admin can read another user's asset content without that user's SecureCard. This dashboard reflects that architecture: presence + intent, never substance.

Events

Post your own events — listening parties, gallery openings, IP showcases, songwriter rounds, hackathons — and discover what other WireIP creators are running. Connect a popular external event app (Eventbrite, Meetup, Luma, Partiful) so people can RSVP through their tool of choice.

⌬ Sentinel Soldiers

Chat with AI Sentinel agents for creative direction, problem-solving, and operational guidance. Pick a specialist · ask questions by text or voice · get spoken answers back. Conversations are local to this device.

⚠ Not professional advice. Sentinel offers strategy and creative direction only. For legal, financial, medical, or tax matters, consult a licensed professional.

Pick a specialist
Pick a Sentinel to begin
Choose an agent on the left · each has a different specialty.

Find Users

Discover other WireIP users currently online on your relay. Tap a user to start a message thread. Your own display name, business name, and Unique ID are broadcast so others can find you · turn off "Public Profile" in Settings to opt out.

Wallet & Stablecoins

Connect a Web3 wallet to pay collaborators, hire service providers, and execute smart-contract escrows. WireIP settles all transactions in audited stablecoins. The protocol is designed to support multiple stablecoin rails ·USDC, USDT, USDP, and additional stablecoins as they achieve institutional-grade audit status and network adoption. No single stablecoin represents more than 50% of the lending pool at any time, ensuring the network is never exposed to a single-issuer depeg event. $WIP is the underlying hard-asset commodity backing tokenized IP value. In the event of a stablecoin failure or depeg, the protocol auto-deploys $WIP to settle outstanding obligations at par ·ensuring every tokenized creator is made whole regardless of stablecoin market conditions.

Connect a Wallet

Pick a provider. WireIP never holds your private keys · connections are read/sign-only and routed through SecureNode where available.

Quick Actions

Recent Payments

Service Marketplace

Hire vetted creative professionals · photographers, songwriters, designers, coders, developers, videographers, musicians, producers, artists. All work is escrowed in stablecoins under a smart-contract agreement; AGI Sentinel reviews every contract for unfair clauses and bad-faith language before signature. Payment auto-releases on milestone delivery.

IP for Sale & Auction

Buy, sell, and auction full ownership of CBIA™ tokenized IP. Listings include fixed-price sales, timed auctions, and exclusive license rights. All transactions settle in stablecoin via smart-contract escrow with AGI Sentinel review.

Smart Contracts

Programmable agreements for service work, licensing, NDAs, royalty splits, and collaboration. AGI Sentinel auto-reviews every draft, scoring fairness and flagging asymmetric terms, ambiguous wording, hidden kill-switches, and bad-faith language before any party signs.

Neural Net IP Scanner

AGI Sentinel™ neural network scans the open internet · search engines, image search, social media, marketplaces, NFT platforms, code repos, AI training datasets, plagiarism databases · for unauthorized uses of any asset in your vault. Matches are scored by perceptual hash, embedding similarity, and metadata correlation.

Continuous Surveillance

Activate the Neural Net to crawl the public internet on a rolling schedule. Sentinel agents compute perceptual hashes (pHash for images, audio fingerprints, code embeddings, text shingles) and compare against your vault's CBIA payloads. Confirmed infringement matches are surfaced here with one-click DMCA / cease-and-desist actions.

⚛ Quantum Resilience Test

Simulates a quantum-computer attack to validate the post-quantum signature backstop. Runs Shor's-algorithm against current Ed25519, then verifies the CRYSTALS-Dilithium + SPHINCS+ hybrid takes over. No production keys touched.

Deploy AI-powered Sentinel Agents to monitor specific surfaces of the network on your behalf. Each agent runs autonomously with read-only access; bonded $WIP returns less the operational fee on recall. Agents never modify state without an explicit human signature.

Affidavits · Sworn Statements

Every time you accept the WireIP Terms of Use, the system stores a formal sworn affidavit · originality, AI disclosure, indemnification, and acceptance of the WireIP DAO Constitution. Each affidavit is hashed with SHA-256, bound to your wallet via Proof-of-Agency, and downloadable as .txt or .pdf for notary use.

SecureNode™ Interface

Phase 1 connects to any compliant SSD (NFC + Secure Element). Phase 2 is the proprietary SecureNode™ SSD with FIPS 140-2 L3, Ti-6Al-4V enclosure, AES-256 NVMe, TPM 2.0.

⛁ SecureNode™ · DAG Bridge Software

The full SecureNode software interface · SecureCard Scanner (NFC · ECDSA P-256) · WireIP DAG Network · SSD Bridge Module · Tokenization Queue · all running locally with no data leaving your device.

🧬 SSD ↔ Network · Code DNA Match

Idle

Scan files on your local SSD vault and cross-match their CBIA hashes against assets tokenized on the WireIP Network. Identical matches surface as already-tokenized deeds, near-matches as similar IP, and for-sale matches as listed on marketplace.

⚜ Order Hardware & Software

OFFICIAL OUTSIDE THE WIRE STORE

Don't have SecureNode™ yet? Order your NFC SecureCard™ for hardware-rooted Proof of Agency, and license the SecureNode™ software interface that bridges this app to your encrypted SSD vault. All orders settle in stablecoin · ships from Atlanta, GA.

⌬
SecureCard™ NFC
Physical Human Gate · MPC Setup
Tamper-evident NFC card with on-card Ed25519 key. Required for all minting, governance, and treasury actions. FIPS 140-2 L3 secure element, ISO/IEC 14443.
  • Ed25519 on-card signing
  • FIPS 140-2 Level 3 element
  • Multi-Party Computation trusted setup
  • Physical TAPP gate for all actions
  • Anti-tamper · IP67 rated
$249 USDC · USDT · $WIP
⛁
SecureNode™ Software
SSD Vault Interface · 3 Tiers
The local service that encrypts asset bytes to your SSD and bridges the WireIP DAG. Connects to any compliant SSD (NFC + Secure Element) or proprietary SecureNode™ SSD.
  • AES-256-GCM disk encryption
  • Local API on 127.0.0.1:8443
  • macOS · Windows · Linux
  • BLAKE3 vertex anchoring
  • Auto-updates via signed channel
$0–$1,499 FREE / PRO / ENTERPRISE

My Hardware & Licenses

0 items

SecureSystem™ · The Sovereign Hardware & Software Suite

The complete human-verification layer of the WireIP Network™ ·deployed as an integrated unit. It pairs the SecureCard™ NFC physical authorization instrument with the SecureNode™ software interface, creating an unbreakable chain of human presence, cryptographic identity, and sovereign custody from the moment of creation to the moment of on-chain settlement.

⛁ Launch SecureNode™ Software

v1.0.0

Open the full SecureNode™ DAG-Bridge software in a new tab. The interface includes the live SecureCard Scanner (NFC · ECDSA P-256), WireIP DAG Network 3D globe, SSD Bridge Module, and Connected Assets · Tokenization Queue. Runs entirely on your device · local API only · no cloud dependency.

No AI system, automated script, bot, or remote process can replicate a physical NFC card tap. That physical gate ·enforced at the hardware level ·is what separates the WireIP Network™ from every other IP protocol on the market.

⬢ Live · SSD + SecureCard Handshake

3D · animated

The proprietary SecureNode™ SSD hosts the encrypted vault. The SecureCard™ taps the SSD's NFC handshake panel · Ed25519 challenge signs the CBIA payload · data crosses into the vault as encrypted bytes.

⌬ ULTRA · PBR · BLOOM · MIRROR
SecureNode™ SSD · Ti-6Al-4V SecureCard™ · NFC NFC field Ed25519 payload

⬡ Live · WireIP DAG ↔ Blockchain Settlement

3D · k=6 parents

On the left, a sequential blockchain (one-parent linear). On the right, the WireIP DAG · every new vertex references up to 6 parents in parallel · achieving soft-final in ~4–8s vs blockchain's 10+ minutes. Tokenized deeds settle to the DAG; periodic Merkle commitments anchor back to the chain.

⌬ ULTRA · TUBE EDGES · BLOOM · MIRROR
Blockchain block DAG vertex Tip (just-minted) Cross-anchor

What's Included in the SecureSystem™

⚿ SecureCard™ NFC · The Physical Human Gate

The SecureCard™ is an NFC-enabled physical verification card. Every time a creator initiates a tokenization event, governance action, or protocol interaction, SecureNode™ prompts a SecureCard™ tap. The card signs the transaction payload with its on-card Ed25519 private key via a FIPS 140-2 Level 3 Secure Element ·proving that a human being is physically present and deliberately authorizing the action. Near Field Communication requires deliberate physical contact; it cannot be spoofed remotely or replicated programmatically.

Specifications
  • On-card Ed25519 signing · FIPS 140-2 Level 3 Secure Element
  • Multi-Party Computation (MPC) trusted setup
  • ISO/IEC 14443 NFC · IP67 rated · anti-tamper construction
  • Physical TAPP gate enforced on all minting, governance, and treasury actions

⛁ SecureNode™ Software Interface · The Vault Bridge

SecureNode™ is the local software service that runs on your machine and bridges the WireIP DAG to your encrypted SSD vault. It connects to any user-chosen compliant SSD meeting two protocol requirements: NFC capability and a Secure Element chip. No OTW-manufactured hardware is required. The protocol is hardware-agnostic at the vault layer by design ·consistent with the WireIP UNIX philosophy of openness and portability.

When SecureNode™ links to a compliant SSD, the device's embedded Secure Element stores the user's private keys, signs tokenization transactions, and manages the cryptographic identity of the asset owner within the protocol. The SSD's NFC interface acts as the physical human authorization channel, requiring deliberate physical interaction to authorize any asset registration, governance vote, or protocol transaction.

How SecureNode™ Interacts with the Device
  1. The creator uploads an asset file. SecureNode™ generates a SHA-256 hash of the asset locally in the browser ·original bytes never leave the device unencrypted.
  2. SecureNode™ wraps the asset payload with AES-256-GCM encryption using a key derived from the Secure Element on the compliant SSD.
  3. The encrypted payload is written to the SSD vault's encrypted NVMe storage. CBIA™ metadata hashes and Merkle proofs are stored locally and linked to the WireIP DAG via deterministic key derivation.
  4. When the creator initiates a mint, SecureNode™ issues a challenge to the SecureCard™ via NFC. The SecureCard™ signs the CBIA payload hash with its on-card Ed25519 private key ·this is Proof of Agency™.
  5. SecureNode™ assembles the Signed Intent Packet (SIP): the asset hash, metadata, PoA™ signature, hardware-verified timestamp, and author public key. The SIP is a legally and cryptographically complete Digital Deed.
  6. When connectivity is available, SecureNode™ broadcasts the SIP to the nearest WireIP DAG node. The DAG verifies the Ed25519 signature and PoA™ timestamp, anchors the vertex with k=6 parent references, and changes the deed status from Local Pending to Global Finalized.
  7. If connectivity is unavailable, the SIP remains stored on the SSD vault and broadcasts automatically at the next connectivity window ·via satellite, mesh, or terrestrial connection. The act of creation is captured at the moment of human authorization, not at the moment of internet connection.
Specifications
  • AES-256-GCM disk encryption
  • Local API on 127.0.0.1:8443 ·no data leaves the device without user initiation
  • Compatible with macOS · Windows · Linux
  • BLAKE3 vertex anchoring to the WireIP DAG
  • Auto-updates via signed firmware channel
  • No passive broadcast or emission ·all sync is user-initiated

Phase Architecture

Phase 1 · Genesis (Now Active)

SecureNode™ operates as a software interface connecting to any user-chosen compliant SSD with NFC and a Secure Element. No proprietary hardware required. Any creator anywhere can participate as a full network node with complete on-chain rights, CBIA™ minting capability, IP staking access, and governance participation from day one.

Phase 2 · Forging (Coming)

The proprietary SecureNode™ SSD is a purpose-built sovereign hardware vault ·Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 titanium enclosure, gold-plated, EMP-resistant, FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM module, AES-256 encrypted NVMe (512 GB), embedded NFC, TPM 2.0 attestation chip, standalone battery, and full air-gap capability. It is an upgrade, not a requirement. Phase 1 creators using their own compliant SSDs are full and equal network participants in every respect.

The SecureCard™ physical NFC verification instrument is also deployed in Phase 2 alongside the proprietary hardware.

IP Registration Status

Live status of every Sentinel-dispatched filing, every locally-recorded registration, and every CBIA™ deed minted to the WireIP DAG. Filter by status, jurisdiction, or asset type · click any row for the full audit trail.

Settings

Personalize WireIP. Preferences save instantly to this browser. Wallet, vault, and SecureNode credentials are managed in their own views.
⌬

Your WireIP Identity

Pinned
⚡ Public handle · how other users find you
@—
Pick your handle in the Profile section below. This is the public part of your identity · safe to share.

Your SecureCard™ Unique ID is generated by your SecureNode™ software when you provision an NFC tag. It's the private credential that authorizes deployments, vault writes, and cross-device sync. Never share it. Hidden by default · use the eye button to reveal.

· awaiting SecureNode provisioning ·
◆ Sourced from SecureNode™ ⛨ Same as SecureCard™ NFC ID ⌬ Used for Proof of Agency™
● SecureNode™ Status
Disconnected · using test code

Tap to handshake with your SecureNode™ at 127.0.0.1:8443. On success, the permanent Unique ID replaces the rotating test code and is pinned for the life of your SecureCard.

⚡ Test Code · rotates every 30 s Testnet

When you use it. Only when verifying a submitted asset in your vault · paste this code (or your Unique ID) to unlock the Deploy to DAG button.
Why it rotates. The code regenerates automatically every 30 seconds so a screenshot or over-the-shoulder glance can't be reused later. Once SecureNode provisions your permanent ID, the test code is retired.

· generating · 30s
⌬

Admin Mode · Network Observer

Restricted feature. Master-account view of network-wide relay traffic. Surfaces what users broadcast publicly — presence, events, asset deployments, marketplace activity — never their IP assets, vault contents, or private metadata. Per WireIP architecture this is technically impossible (per-user data is encrypted to each user's SecureNode and never leaves their device in plaintext).

⌬

Devices

Every device that signed in with your @handle + Unique ID shows up here. Pull your assets onto a new phone or laptop by signing in there — devices already online on your relay will sync state to it automatically.

⚜

User & Business Profile

The display name and business name below populate the Creator field on every new asset and let other users find your portfolio by name in the global search. The legal name, tax ID, email, and phone are stored locally and never indexed in search results.

Business Filing Codes · auto-fill on every IP application
Social Links · used throughout the system

Appearance

♪

Sound

The orb reads only when this is "Always on" or you tap it. We never replay the consent prompt unless you reset it.

Voice availability depends on your operating system. macOS / iOS ship with the richest set (Samantha, Daniel, Ava, Karen). Chrome adds Google voices, Edge adds Microsoft Neural voices. The list below is filtered to high-quality / neural voices only.

⛨

Privacy & Security

Controls how new assets are encrypted, what metadata is published on the DAG, and what survives off your device. Maximum is the default · every field private, every uploaded file stripped of EXIF / camera / location data, every description encrypted before storage.

Identity · Cross-Device Sign-In
Anonymous

Sign in once · access your vault, drafts, and profile from any device. WireIP only stores your email, name, and a verified Google subject ID · we never see your password. Turn KYC off for top-secret operations.

⚙ Advanced · KYC settings & bring-your-own OAuth Client ID

WireIP ships with a default public Google Client ID. For institutional deployments, paste your own Client ID from console.cloud.google.com below so users sign in against your domain.

Not verified · click "Continue with Google" to link a verified identity to this device
Sworn Statement · Terms of Use

Every mint requires you to swear that all submitted information is factual + original, and that any AI assistance is cited. Fully-AI-generated works can only be uploaded as public reference material under CC0 and never tokenized as private IP. Acceptance is timestamped + bound to your wallet via PoA.

Not yet accepted · you will be prompted at your next mint
⚜

Tokenization Defaults

Pre-fill these on every new asset. You can still override per-asset on the form.

⌖

Document & Handwriting Scan

When you upload a physical-document photo or scan, the OCR engine reads its text · including handwriting · using a forensic-document-examiner pipeline (grayscale → adaptive threshold → 2× upscale → Tesseract LSTM with two passes). Pick the language to dramatically improve accuracy.

Wallet & Blockchain

SecureNode™

SecureCard™ NFC Tag

Your physical SecureCard™ is identified by a unique ID baked into an encrypted URL on the NFC tag. The URL itself is never displayed or copyable inside WireIP · only the tag carries it. Re-generate only if your tag is lost or stolen.

Not yet provisioned · open the NFC modal once to generate
⌫

Deleted Assets · Retention

Deleted assets move to a recovery folder. They permanently purge after the retention window. Restore any time before purge from the Deleted Assets view.

⚐

Notifications

⎙

Data & Backup

Cross-Device Sync · WireIP Sync Code

No backend account is needed to move your vault between devices. Generate Sync Code on the device that has your data; you'll get a short URL with an encoded snapshot of all your assets / drafts / settings. Open that URL on your other device and tap Accept Sync · the data lands instantly. Codes expire after 24 hours.

Real-Time Marketplace Relay · multi-user

Without a relay, every event broadcasts only across tabs of the same browser on your machine. Paste a WebSocket URL (wss://) below to connect to a shared relay so messages, jobs, collab requests, and valuation updates flow in real time between every user on the network.

▣

Top Bar Display

Choose what the always-visible chip in the top bar shows. Click the chip itself to cycle between options.

⚿

Lock & Save Preferences

Every change above auto-saves to this browser the moment you make it. Use Lock & Save below to formally confirm the current configuration ·a timestamp is recorded, the banner flips to Locked · Confirmed, and a verify chime fires. Unlocking re-enables idle editing without losing values.

Status: Unlocked · Preferences saving live to this browser
⚠

Danger Zone

Erase ALL local data · assets, wallet, SecureNode credentials, vault drafts, preferences. Use only when starting fresh on this device. This cannot be undone.

Deleted Assets

Assets you have deleted from the vault. They remain here for the retention window set in Settings (default: 30 days), then auto-purge. You can restore any time before that.

Recovery Folder

WireIP Network™ · Specifications

Reference card distilled from the Black Paper.

Vertex Schema

vertex_idBLAKE3 hash
parent_refs[V1, V2, … up to k=6]
payload_typeCBIA_MINT, WIP_TX, POHAW, POA, ROYALTY, GOV_VOTE, LENDING, TREASURY, SECURE_CARD, VERTEX_TIP
poa_signatureEd25519 (NFC SecureCard™)
stake_weight$WIP locked

Consensus & Throughput

typeDAG Async BFT
soft final~4–8 seconds (k=6)
hard final~12–20 seconds
tps target5,000+

$WIP Tokenomics (100M)

Creator Genesis Rewards30% · 30,000,000 $WIP
Creator & Contractor Rewards25% · 25,000,000 $WIP
Public Sale & Liquidity20% · 20,000,000 $WIP
Treasury Reserve (7-of-12 multisig)10% · 10,000,000 $WIP
Strategic Investors10% · 10,000,000 $WIP
Team & Advisors5% · 5,000,000 $WIP
Anti-whale cap (Genesis Phase)5% per wallet · 1,500,000 $WIP max
SIP → Offline Finality → Sync → Global Final Ready Not saved © 2026 Outside The Wire Productions · v4.1 · build ·

Terms · Sworn Statement

WireIP Network™ Terms of Use

By minting a CBIA™ Digital Deed, you agree to the terms below. WireIP records every deed on a public DAG; the statements you make here are bound to your wallet, your Proof-of-Agency signature, and the timestamp of the mint.

1 · Originality & Truth

You swear, under penalty of perjury and the WireIP DAO Constitution, that every piece of information you submit · titles, dates, descriptions, ownership claims, jurisdictions, proof-of-value figures, registration numbers · is factual and true to the best of your knowledge.

You swear that the underlying IP asset is your original creation, that you hold the legal authority to tokenize it, and that no third party retains conflicting ownership unless explicitly disclosed in the Ownership Proof step.

2 · AI Disclosure (REQUIRED)

If any portion of the work was created with AI assistance · including but not limited to: text generation, image generation, audio composition, code generation, vocal synthesis · you must cite the AI tool and disclose what fraction of the work it produced. AI-assisted works ARE tokenizable as long as the human author's substantial contribution can be identified.

Fully AI-generated works · with no substantial human authorship · cannot be tokenized as private IP on WireIP. Such works may only be uploaded as public reference material under a CC0 / public-domain license. Attempts to tokenize fully AI-generated work as private IP are an act of fraud and grounds for permanent deed cancellation by the WireIP DAO assembly.

3 · Indemnification

You hold WireIP Network™, Outside The Wire Productions, the WireIP DAO, and every validator harmless against any claim arising from the falsity of statements you make at mint time. If a third party successfully proves prior ownership, the deed transfers automatically via the kill-switch mechanism you select in Step 6 of tokenization.

4 · Backstop & Treasury

Tokenized IP is backed by the WireIP Treasury's hard-asset reserves; in the event of stablecoin failure or depeg, $WIP auto-deploys at par. The exact backstop mechanics are described in the Black Paper. Treasury custody, validator stake-weighting, and governance follow the WireIP DAO Constitution v1.

5 · Privacy & Custody

You retain custody of the underlying asset bytes. WireIP only records the hash, the metadata you choose to publish, and the deed certificate. Your SecureNode™ vault is the canonical store. WireIP cannot recover lost keys; the WireIP DAO assembly governs key-rotation under tightly-defined conditions only.

6 · Acceptance

Tap "Accept & Mint" only if every sworn statement above is true. Your acceptance is timestamped, hashed, and bound to your Proof-of-Agency signature on every subsequent deed.

Tap Your SecureCard™

        
Hold your NFC SecureCard™ against the reader to issue a Proof-of-Agency™ challenge. The card will sign the CBIA payload with its on-card Ed25519 key.

        
⌬ AWAITING NFC TAP · CHALLENGE:  ·

        

      

      
      

        
          ⌬ Verify with your Unique ID  ·  or paste a tag URL
        
        

          

            Two ways to verify.
            Paste your Unique ID from Settings (format WI-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX) — this is the same identifier programmed onto your physical SecureCard™ tag.
            Or paste the encrypted URL read from a physical tag by an NFC reader app. Either input is wiped immediately on submit and the result is shown without echoing the bytes back.
